summary: Implement secure, non-guessable download URLs by assigning each uploaded file a permanent short random ID and disabling legacy predictable numeric URLs (e.g., d/1).
architectural_notes: Use a permanent short random string as the file's public download identifier (generated on upload and stored with the file record).
Deprecate/disable legacy numeric download routes (e.g., /d/1) so old predictable URLs no longer resolve.
known_issues: Download URLs are currently predictable (e.g., d/1), making them guessable; needs replacement with permanent short random IDs and legacy URLs disabled.

existing_features.title: Ensure the frontend continues to generate and display public download links using the backend-provided token (no client-side token generation), and that copied links use the format /d/{token}.

new_feature_requests.summary: Generate permanent short random download IDs for files and disable legacy numeric URLs (e.g., d/1) so links cannot be guessed.
Update the backend to assign each uploaded file a permanent, short, random public download token (not derived from the numeric file ID), and use this token for all public downloads.
Disable legacy predictable numeric download URLs so that old tokens like "1" no longer work for public downloads, even if they exist in state.

projectName: Use permanent short random download tokens and disable legacy numeric download URLs
